display.purpose: Righteous — hero balances, the treemap's hero percentage. Never body copy, never a category name.

body.purpose: Plus Jakarta Sans — default UI copy, list rows, descriptions.

label.purpose: Plus Jakarta Sans bold uppercase tracked — section eyebrows, treemap category labels, month headers.

shadows.name: floating-chrome
shadows.purpose: Bottom nav pill — the one surviving glass surface.

shadows.name: treemap-text-shadow
shadows.purpose: Uniform legibility helper for white text on any saturated category color — not an elevation shadow, a text-shadow.

motion.name: ff-card-glow
motion.value: @keyframes cycling box-shadow color through Flow Green -> Cyan -> Violet -> Mint, 16s ease-in-out infinite
motion.purpose: Lock screen card only — the one animated shadow in the app. Frozen under prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: treemap-grow-in
motion.value: opacity/scale spring-eased entrance, 40ms stagger per tile
motion.purpose: Treemap tiles on mount / type toggle.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Strongest call to action — save, confirm, primary nav.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #A8FF3E; color: #020203; border: none; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 10px 16px; font-weight: 700; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; transition: transform 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.95); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #020203; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Dark Button
components.description: Secondary strong action, e.g. "All transactions" — theme-aware, not a fixed hex.
components.css: .ds-btn-dark { background: hsl(var(--foreground)); color: hsl(var(--background)); border: none; border-radius: 16px; padding: 16px; font-weight: 700; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; transition: opacity 0.15s; } .ds-btn-dark:active { opacity: 0.8; }

components.name: Expense/Income Toggle
components.description: Flat pill toggle with a sliding colored thumb — no glass. Used in EntrySheet and the treemap header.
components.css: .ds-toggle { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; background: hsl(var(--muted)); padding: 4px; } .ds-toggle-thumb { position: absolute; top: 4px; left: 4px; bottom: 4px; width: calc(50% - 4px); border-radius: 9999px; background: #FF4D4D; } .ds-toggle-btn { position: relative; z-index: 1; padding: 6px 16px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; background: transparent; border: none; color: hsl(var(--foreground) / 0.5); } .ds-toggle-btn.ds-toggle-active { color: #fff; }

components.name: Category Pill (selected)
components.description: Filled with the category's raw color; text color chosen for contrast (readableTextColor helper).
components.html: <button class="ds-pill"><svg class="ds-pill-check" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="3"><path d="M20 6 9 17l-5-5"/></svg>Groceries</button>
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 8px 14px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; background: #EF4444; color: #020203; border: none; } .ds-pill-check { width: 12px; height: 12px; }

components.name: Treemap Tile
components.description: Flat, fully saturated category color; percentage is the hero number, name is a small label above it.
components.css: .ds-tile { display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: space-between; background: #EF4444; color: #f9f8f8; border-radius: 14px; padding: 8px; height: 140px; width: 140px; text-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,.45); } .ds-tile-name { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 800; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; opacity: 0.9; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; } .ds-tile-pct { font-family: 'Righteous', sans-serif; font-size: 42px; line-height: 0.9; }

narrative.overview:
Flow! used to put a saturated gradient behind every card — Balance, Expense, Income, Insights each lived in its own pastel-to-vivid zone. That system has been retired. The current interface is deliberately quiet at rest: pages are unstyled neutral surfaces (no card chrome, no gradients, no drop shadows on content), numbers sit directly on the page background, and rows are separated by hairline borders instead of boxes. Voltage — saturated, vivid color — is not gone, it's been concentrated into the moments that earn it: the number itself (Righteous display type, never the container around it), the category treemap (flat pure hues, the loudest color surface in the app), the biometric lock screen's aurora mesh, and Flow Green as the one action color that crosses every screen. This is a Restrained-base, Committed-moments strategy: most of the UI is Restrained (neutral surface + one accent), but two or three signature surfaces per screen are allowed to go fully Committed.

This is a real departure from PRODUCT.md's original framing ("colores saturados y luminosos como identidad central, no un acento aislado") — the base UI today is closer to an isolated-accent model than the original brief called for. The counter-argument that's held up through many rounds of user iteration: the identity now lives in a few unmistakable, high-craft moments rather than being smeared across every surface, and the user has consistently chosen this direction when shown both.
keyCharacteristics: Content surfaces are flat and borderless at rest: no card background, no drop shadow, no gradient. Sections separate with a hairline border, never a boxed container.
Righteous (the "numbers get personality" display face) is the one place saturated color and display type meet: hero balances, the treemap's hero percentage, deltas.
Color as data, not decoration: category colors, income/expense deltas, and toggle states are the only places hue carries meaning outside the two signature moments below.
Two signature "loud" surfaces, deliberately rare: the biometric lock screen's four-color static aurora mesh, and the dashboard's category treemap.
Floating chrome (bottom nav) is the one surviving piece of liquid glass.
Flow Green is the one color present on every screen.

rules.name: The Semantic Temperature Rule
rules.body: Green means income/positive, red means expense/negative. The exact hue can shift; the meaning never inverts, never goes gray.

rules.name: The Numbers-Only Voltage Rule
rules.body: Saturated color on a content surface is earned by being a number (Righteous display type) or a category swatch — never a decorative card background.

rules.name: The Shadow-Not-Gradient Rule for legibility
rules.body: When white text needs to sit on an arbitrary saturated category color, the fix is a single soft text-shadow applied uniformly — never a per-color gradient, never a 4-direction stroke shadow.

rules.name: The Numbers-Get-Personality Rule
rules.body: Righteous is reserved for figures the user cares about. Body copy, labels, and category names stay in Plus Jakarta Sans, even inside the treemap right next to a Righteous number.

rules.name: The Hairline, Not Card Rule
rules.body: Where the old system would have reached for a card + shadow to separate content, the current system reaches for a hairline border first. A card is the exception (the lock screen), not the default container.

dos: Do default every new page/section to a flat, borderless surface — hairlines between sections, not cards.
Do let Righteous carry the loudest color in any given screen; keep everything else in Plus Jakarta Sans regardless of background saturation.
Do keep Flow Green as the one cross-app action color, paired with near-black text.
Do use a single soft text-shadow (never a gradient, never a 4-direction stroke) when white text needs to sit on an arbitrary saturated color.
Do keep green = income/positive and red = expense/negative absolute.
donts: Don't put a gradient or tint behind a content card "for energy" — that's the retired system.
Don't reuse the aurora mesh outside the lock screen.
Don't name a treemap's aggregate/overflow bucket "Other" — use a count-based label instead (+N more).
Don't invert or neutralize the income/expense color coding.
Don't default to a cream/beige body background "for warmth" — background stays a low-chroma near-white/near-black.
